name: Windows API / Devices structured pack
description: Structured Win32 GUI, diagnostics, networking, device, registry, IPC, filesystem, shared-memory, synchronization, secure DLL-loading, volume, job-object, IP Helper and PSAPI APIs with guided selectors, native path browsers and editable structures.

functions.signature: HWND CreateWindowEx(DWORD dwExStyle, LPCTSTR lpClassName, LPCTSTR lpWindowName, DWORD dwStyle, int X, int Y, int nWidth, int nHeight, HWND hWndParent, HMENU hMenu, HINSTANCE hInstance, LPVOID lpParam);
functions.insertText: CreateWindowEx(0, TEXT("MyWindowClass"), TEXT("My application"), WS_OVERLAPPEDWINDOW | WS_VISIBLE, CW_USEDEFAULT, CW_USEDEFAULT, 900, 600, NULL, NULL, hInstance, NULL);
functions.description: Create a top-level or child window.
functions.longDescription: Create a top-level or child window.

parameters.name: dwExStyle
parameters.description: Bitmask flags. Use the advanced selection window (…) to inspect and combine documented constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select flags for CreateWindowEx.dwExStyle
pickerConfig.subtitle: Extended window styles can be combined when their semantics are compatible.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

groups.label: Extended window styles
groups.description: Common WS_EX_* constants accepted by CreateWindowEx.

items.label: WS_EX_ACCEPTFILES
items.description: The window accepts files dragged from the shell.

items.label: WS_EX_APPWINDOW
items.description: Forces a top-level window onto the taskbar when visible.

items.label: WS_EX_CLIENTEDGE
items.description: Gives the window a sunken client-edge border.

items.label: WS_EX_COMPOSITED
items.description: Paints descendants bottom-to-top using double buffering.

items.label: WS_EX_CONTEXTHELP
items.description: Adds a question-mark help button to the title bar when compatible with the other styles.

items.label: WS_EX_CONTROLPARENT
items.description: Allows keyboard navigation among child controls, including TAB traversal.

items.label: WS_EX_LAYERED
items.description: Creates a layered window for opacity or per-pixel alpha effects.

items.label: WS_EX_NOACTIVATE
items.description: Prevents a top-level window from becoming the foreground window when clicked.

items.label: WS_EX_NOPARENTNOTIFY
items.description: Prevents WM_PARENTNOTIFY from being sent to the parent when a child is created or destroyed.

items.label: WS_EX_STATICEDGE
items.description: Adds a three-dimensional border suitable for items that do not accept input.

items.label: WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW
items.description: Creates a floating toolbar-style window with a smaller title bar and no normal taskbar button.

items.label: WS_EX_TOPMOST
items.description: Keeps the window above non-topmost windows until the z-order is changed.

items.label: WS_EX_TRANSPARENT
items.description: Paints siblings beneath the window before painting the window itself.

parameters.name: dwStyle
parameters.description: Bitmask flags. Use the advanced selection window (…) to inspect and combine documented constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select flags for CreateWindowEx.dwStyle
pickerConfig.subtitle: Choose a coherent base window type, then add compatible visibility, clipping or control-navigation styles.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

groups.label: Base window styles
groups.description: Common WS_* constants. Some style combinations are mutually exclusive; keep the semantic window type coherent.

items.label: WS_OVERLAPPEDWINDOW
items.description: Convenience combination for a standard overlapped top-level window with caption, system menu, sizing border and minimize/maximize boxes.

items.label: WS_POPUP
items.description: Creates a pop-up window. Do not combine this with WS_CHILD.

items.label: WS_CHILD
items.description: Creates a child window. Do not combine this with WS_POPUP.

items.label: WS_CLIPSIBLINGS
items.description: Clips sibling child windows when painting.

items.label: WS_CLIPCHILDREN
items.description: Excludes child-window areas when painting inside the parent.

items.label: WS_TABSTOP
items.description: Allows keyboard focus through TAB navigation.

items.label: WS_GROUP
items.description: Marks the first control in a group of controls.

parameters.description: Guided constant selection. Use the list or the advanced window (…) to inspect documented values.

options.label: SW_HIDE
options.description: Hides the window and activates another window.

options.label: SW_SHOWNORMAL
options.description: Activates and displays the window. Restores the original size and position when minimized or maximized.

options.label: SW_SHOW
options.description: Activates and displays the window using its current size and position.

options.label: SW_SHOWNA
options.description: Displays the window using its current size and position without activating it.

options.label: SW_SHOWDEFAULT
options.description: Uses the show state supplied in STARTUPINFO by the process launcher.

options.label: SW_MINIMIZE
options.description: Minimizes the specified window and activates the next top-level window.

options.label: SW_RESTORE
options.description: Activates and restores a minimized or maximized window.

groups.description: Combine the class behaviors required by RegisterClassEx.

items.label: CS_VREDRAW
items.description: Redraw the entire window when the client-area height changes.

items.label: CS_HREDRAW
items.description: Redraw the entire window when the client-area width changes.

items.label: CS_DBLCLKS
items.description: Send double-click messages to the window procedure.

items.label: CS_OWNDC
items.description: Allocate a unique device context for each window in the class.

items.label: CS_CLASSDC
items.description: Share one device context between windows in the class.

items.label: CS_PARENTDC
items.description: Use the parent device context clipping rectangle.

items.label: CS_NOCLOSE
items.description: Disable Close on the window menu.

items.label: CS_SAVEBITS
items.description: Save the obscured screen area as a bitmap when practical.

items.label: CS_DROPSHADOW
items.description: Enable the drop-shadow effect for supported top-level windows.

functions.name: SetWindowPos
functions.signature: BOOL SetWindowPos(HWND hWnd, HWND hWndInsertAfter, int X, int Y, int cx, int cy, UINT uFlags);
functions.description: Adjust z-order, position, visibility, or size of a window.
functions.longDescription: Adjust z-order, position, visibility, or size of a window.

parameters.name: hWnd
parameters.description: Handle, object or pointer consumed by SetWindowPos.

parameters.name: hWndInsertAfter
parameters.description: Handle, object or pointer consumed by SetWindowPos.

parameters.name: uFlags
parameters.description: Bitmask flags. Use the advanced selection window (…) to inspect and combine documented constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select flags for SetWindowPos.uFlags
pickerConfig.subtitle: Combine geometry, activation, visibility and repaint behavior flags. The details panel explains each selected constant.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

groups.label: Position and size
groups.description: Flags controlling which geometry values SetWindowPos applies.

items.label: SWP_NOMOVE
items.description: Retains the current position and ignores the X and Y parameters.

items.label: SWP_NOSIZE
items.description: Retains the current size and ignores the cx and cy parameters.

items.label: SWP_NOZORDER
items.description: Retains the current z-order and ignores hWndInsertAfter.

items.label: SWP_NOOWNERZORDER
items.description: Does not change the owner window position in the z-order.

items.label: SWP_NOACTIVATE
items.description: Does not activate the window. If omitted, the window can be activated and moved to the top of the relevant z-order group.

groups.label: Painting and notifications
groups.description: Flags affecting repainting and window-position notifications.

items.label: SWP_FRAMECHANGED
items.description: Applies new frame styles set with SetWindowLong and sends WM_NCCALCSIZE even when the size does not change.

items.label: SWP_DRAWFRAME
items.description: Draws a frame around the window. This is the same value as SWP_FRAMECHANGED.

items.label: SWP_NOREDRAW
items.description: Does not redraw changes. The caller must invalidate or redraw affected regions explicitly.

items.label: SWP_NOCOPYBITS
items.description: Discards the existing client-area contents instead of preserving them after movement or resize.

items.label: SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS
items.description: Posts the request to the owning thread when the caller and target window are attached to different input queues.

presets.description: Update z-order without moving, resizing or activating
Recalculate the non-client frame after style changes

functions.signature: BOOL RedrawWindow(HWND hWnd, const RECT *lprcUpdate, HRGN hrgnUpdate, UINT flags);
functions.description: Force a redraw of a window with fine-grained update flags.
functions.longDescription: Force a redraw of a window with fine-grained update flags.

parameters.name: hWnd
parameters.description: Handle, object or pointer consumed by RedrawWindow.

parameters.name: lprcUpdate
parameters.description: Lprc Update argument passed to RedrawWindow (const RECT *).

parameters.description: Bitmask flags. Use the advanced selection window (…) to inspect and combine documented constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select flags for RedrawWindow.flags
pickerConfig.subtitle: Combine invalidation, validation, timing and child-window flags.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

groups.label: Invalidation
groups.description: Flags controlling which region is invalidated or validated.

items.label: RDW_INVALIDATE
items.description: Invalidates the update region. A WM_PAINT message is posted when the update region is not empty.

items.label: RDW_ERASE
items.description: Causes the window to receive WM_ERASEBKGND when repainting.

items.label: RDW_FRAME
items.description: Invalidates the nonclient area when combined with RDW_INVALIDATE.

items.label: RDW_INTERNALPAINT
items.description: Posts WM_PAINT regardless of whether the update region is empty.

groups.label: Timing and children
groups.description: Flags controlling immediate redraw and traversal of child windows.

items.label: RDW_UPDATENOW
items.description: Immediately sends WM_NCPAINT, WM_ERASEBKGND and WM_PAINT before returning when needed.

items.label: RDW_ERASENOW
items.description: Immediately sends WM_NCPAINT and WM_ERASEBKGND before returning when needed.

items.label: RDW_NOCHILDREN
items.description: Excludes child windows from the redraw operation.

items.label: RDW_ALLCHILDREN
items.description: Includes child windows in the redraw operation.

presets.description: Invalidate window and children, including background erase

pickerConfig.subtitle: Select one button layout, at most one icon, an optional default button and compatible behavior flags.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

items.label: MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE
items.description: Displays Abort, Retry and Ignore buttons.

items.label: MB_YESNOCANCEL
items.description: Displays Yes, No and Cancel buttons.

items.label: MB_CANCELTRYCONTINUE
items.description: Displays Cancel, Try Again and Continue buttons.

groups.label: Icons
groups.description: Select at most one icon constant.

items.label: MB_ICONERROR
items.description: Displays the stop-sign icon. Same value as MB_ICONHAND and MB_ICONSTOP.

items.label: MB_ICONQUESTION
items.description: Displays a question-mark icon. Avoid this icon when a clearer semantic icon is available.

items.label: MB_ICONWARNING
items.description: Displays an exclamation-point warning icon. Same value as MB_ICONEXCLAMATION.

items.label: MB_ICONINFORMATION
items.description: Displays an information icon. Same value as MB_ICONASTERISK.

groups.label: Default button
groups.description: Select at most one default-button position.

items.label: MB_DEFBUTTON1
items.description: Makes the first button the default.

items.label: MB_DEFBUTTON2
items.description: Makes the second button the default.

items.label: MB_DEFBUTTON3
items.description: Makes the third button the default.

items.label: MB_DEFBUTTON4
items.description: Makes the fourth button the default.

items.label: MB_APPLMODAL
items.description: Blocks input to windows owned by the current thread until the message box is closed.

items.label: MB_SYSTEMMODAL
items.description: Places the message box above other windows. Use only for serious conditions.

items.label: MB_TOPMOST
items.description: Creates the message box with the WS_EX_TOPMOST style.

items.label: MB_SETFOREGROUND
items.description: Makes the message box the foreground window.

items.label: MB_RTLREADING

items.description: Interprets uIDNewItem as the handle of a submenu.

items.description: Creates an owner-drawn item and sends WM_MEASUREITEM and WM_DRAWITEM to the owner.

items.description: Enables the menu item so it can be selected.

items.description: Disables and grays the menu item.

items.label: MF_DISABLED
items.description: Disables the menu item without graying it.

items.label: MF_CHECKED
items.description: Places a check mark next to the menu item.

items.label: MF_UNCHECKED
items.description: Does not place a check mark next to the menu item.

items.label: MF_MENUBREAK
items.description: Places the item on a new line for menu bars or in a new column for pop-up menus.

items.label: MF_MENUBARBREAK

items.description: Positions the pop-up menu so its right side aligns with x.

items.label: TPM_TOPALIGN
items.description: Positions the pop-up menu so its top side aligns with y.

items.label: TPM_VCENTERALIGN
items.description: Centers the pop-up menu vertically on y.

items.description: Positions the pop-up menu so its bottom side aligns with y.

items.label: TPM_RIGHTBUTTON
items.description: Lets the user select menu items with either mouse button.

items.label: TPM_NONOTIFY
items.description: Prevents notification messages when the user clicks an item.

items.label: TPM_RETURNCMD
items.description: Returns the selected command identifier instead of posting a menu command message.

items.label: TPM_RECURSE
items.description: Allows displaying a pop-up menu while another pop-up menu is already displayed.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

items.label: DT_TOP
items.description: Top-aligns the text for single-line rendering.

items.label: DT_VCENTER
items.description: Centers text vertically. Combine with DT_SINGLELINE.

groups.label: Layout and clipping
groups.description: Flags affecting layout, wrapping and clipping.

items.label: DT_SINGLELINE
items.description: Displays text on a single line. Carriage returns and line feeds do not break the line.

items.label: DT_WORDBREAK
items.description: Breaks lines between words when text exceeds the rectangle width.

items.label: DT_NOCLIP
items.description: Draws without clipping, which can be faster when the text is guaranteed to stay inside the rectangle.

items.label: DT_CALCRECT
items.description: Calculates the rectangle required to draw the text instead of rendering it.

items.label: DT_EDITCONTROL
items.description: Formats text like a multiline edit control.

groups.label: Ellipsis and prefixes
groups.description: Flags for shortened text and ampersand-prefix handling.

items.label: DT_END_ELLIPSIS
items.description: Adds ellipses at the end when the string does not fit.

items.label: DT_PATH_ELLIPSIS
items.description: Replaces characters in the middle with ellipses so path information remains recognizable.

items.label: DT_WORD_ELLIPSIS
items.description: Truncates words that do not fit and adds ellipses.

items.label: DT_NOPREFIX
items.description: Treats ampersands as normal characters instead of accelerator prefixes.

functions.declaration: int DrawText(HDC hDC, LPTSTR lpchText, int cchText, LPRECT lprc, UINT format);
notes: format uses a documented multi-select flag picker. Selected constants are combined with the bitwise OR operator.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_DEFAULT_DIRS
items.description: Use the recommended application, user and System32 directory set.

items.label: LOAD_WITH_ALTERED_SEARCH_PATH
items.description: Use the alternate dependency search strategy. Use an absolute DLL path.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_REQUIRE_SIGNED_TARGET
items.description: Require a valid digital signature for the binary image at load time.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SAFE_CURRENT_DIRS

functions.name: Supervised child-process recipe note
functions.signature: /* CreateJobObjectW -> configure JOBOBJECT_EXTENDED_LIMIT_INFORMATION with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE -> SetInformationJobObject -> CreateProcessW -> AssignProcessToJobObject -> close process and thread handles when no longer needed -> CloseHandle(job) to trigger configured cleanup. */
functions.insertText: /* CreateJobObjectW -> configure JOBOBJECT_EXTENDED_LIMIT_INFORMATION with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE -> SetInformationJobObject -> CreateProcessW -> AssignProcessToJobObject -> close process and thread handles when no longer needed -> CloseHandle(job) to trigger configured cleanup. */
functions.description: Recommended child-worker supervision lifecycle for tools and test benches.
functions.longDescription: Recommended child-worker supervision lifecycle for tools and test benches.
functions.declaration: /* CreateJobObjectW -> configure JOBOBJECT_EXTENDED_LIMIT_INFORMATION with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE -> SetInformationJobObject -> CreateProcessW -> AssignProcessToJobObject -> close process and thread handles when no longer needed -> CloseHandle(job) to trigger configured cleanup. */

functions.name: Minidump diagnostic safety note
functions.signature: /* Minidumps can contain sensitive application state. Store them in a controlled location, restrict access, and collect only the detail level required for debugging. In a crash handler, prefer delegating MiniDumpWriteDump to a separate helper process when feasible. */

functions.name: IP Helper buffer lifecycle note
functions.signature: /* GetAdaptersAddresses, GetNetworkParams and extended TCP/UDP table APIs commonly require a sizing call followed by allocation and a second call. Tables returned through GetIfTable2, GetIpForwardTable2 and GetIpNetTable2 are released with FreeMibTable. Cancel asynchronous change notifications with CancelMibChangeNotify2. */
functions.insertText: /* GetAdaptersAddresses, GetNetworkParams and extended TCP/UDP table APIs commonly require a sizing call followed by allocation and a second call. Tables returned through GetIfTable2, GetIpForwardTable2 and GetIpNetTable2 are released with FreeMibTable. Cancel asynchronous change notifications with CancelMibChangeNotify2. */
functions.description: IP Helper sizing, allocation and cleanup guidance.

Tables returned through GetIfTable2, GetIpForwardTable2 and GetIpNetTable2 are released with FreeMibTable. Cancel asynchronous change notifications with CancelMibChangeNotify2. */

parameters.description: Bitmask flags. Use the advanced selection window (…) to inspect and combine documented constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select flags for PlaySound.fdwSound
pickerConfig.subtitle: Choose how pszSound is interpreted and whether playback is synchronous, asynchronous or looped.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more constants. The generated value is a bitwise OR expression.

groups.label: Sound source
groups.description: Select the source interpretation for pszSound.

items.label: SND_ALIAS
items.description: Interprets pszSound as a system-event alias.

items.label: SND_FILENAME
items.description: Interprets pszSound as a file name.

items.label: SND_MEMORY
items.description: Interprets pszSound as an in-memory WAVE resource.

items.label: SND_RESOURCE
items.description: Interprets pszSound as a resource identifier and requires hmod.

items.label: SND_ASYNC
items.description: Returns immediately and plays the sound asynchronously.

items.label: SND_SYNC
items.description: Plays synchronously and returns when playback ends.

items.label: SND_LOOP
items.description: Repeats the sound until another PlaySound call stops it. Requires SND_ASYNC.

items.label: SND_NODEFAULT
items.description: Does not play the default sound if the requested sound cannot be found.

items.label: SND_NOSTOP
items.description: Does not interrupt a sound that is already playing.

items.label: SND_PURGE
items.description: Stops playback for the specified sound. This flag is not supported on all modern Windows platforms.

items.label: SND_SYSTEM
items.description: Assigns the sound to the system notification audio session.

presets.description: Play a system alias without fallback

functions.declaration: BOOL PlaySound(LPCTSTR pszSound, HMODULE hmod, DWORD fdwSound);
notes: fdwSound uses a documented multi-select flag picker. Selected constants are combined with the bitwise OR operator.

items.label: FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL
items.description: Specifies no other file attributes. Use this constant alone.

items.label: FILE_ATTRIBUTE_SYSTEM
items.description: Marks the file as part of the operating system.

items.label: FILE_ATTRIBUTE_TEMPORARY
items.description: Indicates that the file is used for temporary storage.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED
items.description: Enables asynchronous overlapped I/O. Pass an OVERLAPPED structure to compatible read/write calls.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING
items.description: Disables system buffering and imposes alignment requirements.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_WRITE_THROUGH
items.description: Writes through intermediate caches where possible.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_RANDOM_ACCESS
items.description: Hints that access will be random.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_SEQUENTIAL_SCAN
items.description: Hints that access will be sequential.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_DELETE_ON_CLOSE
items.description: Deletes the file when the last eligible handle closes.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS
items.description: Allows directory handles and backup/restore semantics.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT
items.description: Opens the reparse point itself rather than following it.

items.description: Indicates that the file is used for temporary storage.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED
items.description: Enables asynchronous overlapped I/O. Pass an OVERLAPPED structure to compatible read/write calls.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING
items.description: Disables system buffering and imposes alignment requirements.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_WRITE_THROUGH
items.description: Writes through intermediate caches where possible.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_RANDOM_ACCESS
items.description: Hints that access will be random.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_SEQUENTIAL_SCAN
items.description: Hints that access will be sequential.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_DELETE_ON_CLOSE
items.description: Deletes the file when the last eligible handle closes.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS
items.description: Allows directory handles and backup/restore semantics.

items.label: FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT
items.description: Opens the reparse point itself rather than following it.
